seoTitle: How to Add an Email Signature in Brevo
metaDescription: Embed a polished, responsive email signature in Brevo templates using graphic banners or rich text blocks, complete with social links and alt text.
intro: A personalized sign-off adds a human touch to your outgoing campaigns, but getting it to render correctly across email clients takes some care. Brevo supports both graphic banner signatures and responsive multi-column text signatures through its drag-and-drop builder. This guide covers building one that holds up across devices.

steps.heading: Open your design canvas
steps.body: Launch the active campaign or template canvas where you want to add the signature.

steps.heading: Choose your signature style
steps.body: Decide between a clean graphic banner image or a responsive multi-column text grid, depending on how much detail you want to include.

steps.heading: Position the asset blocks
steps.body: Navigate the left elements library to place your chosen signature block in the correct spot within the email layout.

steps.heading: Style contact text and add social links
steps.body: Format multi-line contact text fields and append social media links if you're using a text-based signature.

steps.heading: Set alt text and validate rendering
steps.body: Add alternate text tags for accessibility, then check cross-platform mobile rendering before sending.

faq.question: Should I use a graphic or text signature?
faq.answer: A graphic banner gives a polished branded look, while a text-based signature tends to render more reliably and loads faster across email clients.

faq.question: Can I add social media icons to my signature?
faq.answer: Yes, social media links can be appended to a text-based signature block.

faq.question: Why should I add alt text to a signature image?
faq.answer: Alt text ensures the signature remains accessible and informative even if images are blocked in a recipient's inbox.

faq.question: Will my signature look the same on mobile as on desktop?
faq.answer: It should if built with responsive elements, but it's still worth validating cross-platform mobile rendering before sending.
